description: This bar chart presents the Vickers hardness (HV) of longitudinal and cross sections of Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel manufactured by selective laser melting (SLM) under different laser powers: 70W (sample P-70), 90W (P-90), 110W (P-110), 130W (P-130), 150W (P-150). The longitudinal section hardness values are 279 HV, 289 HV, 294 HV, 284 HV, 283 HV for P-70 to P-150 respectively, while cross section hardness values are 278 HV, 285 HV, 292 HV, 283 HV, 281 HV for the corresponding samples. Hardness first increases as laser power rises from 70W to 110W, then decreases with further laser power increment, with the P-110 sample exhibiting the maximum hardness, and longitudinal section hardness slightly higher than cross section hardness for all samples. The hardness variation is ascribed to combined effects of grain size and internal defects (pores, cracks, unfused powder particles), verifying that 110W is the optimized laser power for SLM fabrication of this alloy to achieve superior hardness performance.
